programmes.show_description: The workers of Hatley railway station live a gentle and predictable life, but that is rudely interrupted by some alarming news and a new station master. S1 E1: All Change
Gary is left feeling unwanted when a Czech officer takes an interest in Phoebe. S2 E2: I Got It Bad and That Ain't Good
Mr. Bean loses midnight on New Year's Eve, devises an unusual method of transporting his sales shopping home, and wreaks havoc by doing a spot of decorating. E9: Do-It-Yourself, Mr. Bean
Drama series about a detective who wakes up in what seems to be 1973. When CID hear of a dead miner, all roads lead to the notorious cop killer Leslie Johns. Starring John Simm. S2 E8
When Beryl breaks Sandra's Mother's Day present, all does not go according to plan when both sets of parents are invited to spend the evening with their daughters. S2 E11: Mother's Day
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Peggy petitions against the demolition of a disused church, Malcolm has problems at work when receipts go missing, and Brenda has a go at Pamela for trying to set her up with Terry. S2 E5: Missing

Auntie Vera has got problems with her hips and her defective lifts and tells Sharon and Tracey she is moving in - there's only one room available, though. S12 E5: Tonight I'll Be Staying Here With You
Rowan Atkinson stars as the hapless Mr. Bean. In this original award-winning episode, he takes an exam, sunbathes and goes to church with Richard Briers. Ep1: Mr. Bean
David is caught out when Amy tells him that Tommy Wright is dead, so he and Linda adopt disguises to avoid each other's spouses as they plan to move to another hotel. S3 E2: Deja Vu

Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S5, E5: Bottoms Up
The crew of the television holiday programme 'Wish You Were Here' - along with its presenter Judith Chalmers - are at the hotel. S3 E3: Close Up
Alf's cake business goes from strength to strength, and Sir Ralph is convinced that his wife is plotting to poison him. S3 E2 Current Affairs
Rigsby is hoping to take new tenant Brenda out for the evening, but he has a cashflow problem and a string of unwelcome visitors who start arriving at the house. S2 E6: Last of the Big Spenders
Wolfie's not the one on trial for once as he finds himself facing Ronnie Lynch in court. S4 E4: The Letter of the Law
Jack has flown the nest to seek his fortune in California. Errant cousin Shifty is released from prison and comes to stay with Grandad. S5 E1
Sandra is convinced into looking after her grandad and gets Beryl to help her, but it seems that grandad is a whole heap of trouble for them both. S2 E10: Grandad
Harry the signalman has fattened up a lovely turkey in time for Christmas, but the turkey seems to have other plans. S1 E2: Talking Turkey
Alf's cake business goes from strength to strength, and Sir Ralph is convinced that his wife is plotting to poison him. S3 E2 Current Affairs
Robin's brother Norman comes to stay for two days and takes a shine to Chrissy; when the three of them go out to dinner, Robin gets food poisoning. S6 E5: Mum Always Liked You Best
The students prepare to take a mock exam. But when they see that their teacher has no faith in them, they decide to cheat in order to ace the exam. S1 E7: The Cheating Game
Alf and Mrs Hollingbery are now engaged to be married and he has already started to enjoy her cooking. They treat Arthur, who thinks he's won the pools, to a celebration meal... S4 E3
Sandra is convinced into looking after her grandad and gets Beryl to help her, but it seems that grandad is a whole heap of trouble for them both. S2 E10: Grandad

Things start to go wrong for Arthur after he sells an over priced and unroadworthy Austin Allegro to a nun. S10 E5: The Immaculate Contraption

Gary is left feeling unwanted when a Czech officer takes an interest in Phoebe. S2 E3: Just One More Chance
Mr. Bean goes to the funfair and ends up having to watch over a baby; will the mother find them in time? E10: Mind the Baby, Mr. Bean
Drama series following the exploits of old-school detective Gene Hunt from the drama series Life on Mars. DI Alex Drake is shot and lands in 1981 before coming face-to-face with Hunt. S1 E1
Sandra becomes Beryl's boss in a work promotion; to be more like Sandra, Beryl takes elocution lessons and practises on a customer at work which leads to trouble for both girls. S2 E12: Promotion
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Pamela returns from London with David, Malcolm panics after nearly inviting the wrong person to dinner, and Brenda doesn't take kindly to being treated like riff-raff on her birthday. S2 E6: Dining
A surprise visitor in the form of attractive Emma turns up at Tracey's house - Tracey is welcoming, Dorien is shocked to the core, and Travis is over the moon. S12 E6: Abandoned Love
Mr. Bean turns the most ordinary situations into moments of excruciating embarrassment as he visits a department store and gets carried away. E2: The Return of Mr. Bean

Ivy finds out the truth about Alf's orphans, and Miss Cissy decides to go into politics to Lord Meldrum's disgust. S3 E3 Mrs Lipton's Nasty Turn
Could rumours of the Grey Lady who haunts the house be true? Rigsby certainly believes so, but he has not seen her yet... S2 E7: Things That Go Bump In The Night
The TPF - along with Mum and Dad - are in for a long night when they get held up by fugitive Speed and his gun-toting comrade MacTaggart. S4 E5: Prisoners
Cousin Shifty is up to his old criminal tricks which leads to a bust up with Joey. Aveline brings home her 'unsuitable' fiance, Oswald. S5 E2
When Beryl breaks Sandra's Mother's Day present, all does not go according to plan when both sets of parents are invited to spend the evening with their daughters. S2 E11: Mother's Day
New station master Cecil Parkin is intent on making an impression at Hatley, but Jack is sure that he's already made an impression on his wife May. S1 E3: The Train Now Standing

Chrissy and Norman have been dating for some weeks to the annoyance of Robin, who goes on a disastrous double date with Larry. S6 E6: Fire Down Below
Ali and Su-Lee surprise the class when they announce that they will be getting married. S1 E8: Better to Have Loved and Lost
Alf, Mrs Hollingbery and Arthur are preparing for their trip to Australia. S4 E4

Arthur's venture into corporate hospitality takes him and Ray into company that is both glamorous and dangerous. S10 E6: All Quiet on the West End Front
